tools.name: create_complete_workflow
tools.description:
Create a complete workflow with trigger, steps, and connections in a single operation.

CRITICAL SCHEMA REQUIREMENTS:
- Trigger type MUST be one of: DATABASE_EVENT, MANUAL, CRON, WEBHOOK
- NEVER use "RECORD_CREATED" - this is invalid. Use "DATABASE_EVENT" instead.
- Each step MUST include: id (must be a valid UUID), name, type, valid, settings
- CREATE_RECORD actions MUST have objectName and objectRecord in settings.input
- objectRecord must contain actual field values, not just field names
- Use "trigger" as stepId for trigger step in stepPositions and edges

Common mistakes to avoid:
- Using "RECORD_CREATED" instead of "DATABASE_EVENT"
- Missing the "name" and "valid" fields in steps
- Missing the "objectRecord" field in CREATE_RECORD actions
- Using "fieldsToUpdate" instead of "objectRecord" in CREATE_RECORD actions
- Including CODE steps in this tool — this tool does NOT create the underlying logic function needed by CODE steps. Instead, create the workflow without CODE steps first, then add CODE steps individually using create_workflow_version_step (which properly creates the logic function), then call update_logic_function_source to define the code.

IMPORTANT: The tool schema provides comprehensive field descriptions, examples, and validation rules. Always refer to the schema for:
- Field requirements and data types
- Common object patterns and field structures
- Proper relationship field formats
- Variable reference syntax: {{trigger.fieldName}} for trigger data, {{<step-id>.result.fieldName}} for step outputs (step-id is the step's UUID, not its name)
- Error handling options

This is the most efficient way for AI to create workflows as it handles all the complexity in one call.

name.description: The name of the workflow

anyOf.description: Database event trigger that fires when a record is created, updated, deleted, or upserted. The triggered record is accessible in workflow steps via {{trigger.object.fieldName}}.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

eventName.description: Event name in format: objectName.action (e.g., "company.created", "person.updated", "task.deleted", "company.upserted"). Use lowercase object names.

outputSchema.description: Schema defining the output data structure. For database events, this includes the record that triggered the workflow accessible via {{trigger.object.fieldName}}.

anyOf.description: Manual trigger that can be launched by the user. If a record is selected when launched, it is accessible via {{trigger.record.fieldName}}. If no record is selected, no data context is available.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

outputSchema.description: Schema defining the output data structure. When a record is selected, it is accessible via {{trigger.record.fieldName}}. When no record is selected, no data is available.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

id.description: Unique identifier for the workflow step. Must be unique within the workflow.

name.description: Human-readable name for the workflow step. Should clearly describe what the step does.

valid.description: Whether the step configuration is valid. Set to true when all required fields are properly configured.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that this step connects to. Leave empty or null for the final step.

position.description: Position coordinates for the step in the workflow diagram.

logicFunctionId.description: The ID of the logic function that holds the code. This is auto-generated when a CODE step is created via create_workflow_version_step — do NOT set this manually.

logicFunctionInput.description: Key-value map of input parameters to pass to the logic function at runtime.

outputSchema.description: Schema defining the output data structure. This data can be referenced in subsequent steps using {{stepId.fieldName}}.

value.description: Whether to retry the action if it fails.
Whether to continue to the next step if this action fails.

stepPositions.description: Optional array of step positions for layout

stepId.description: The ID of the step (use "trigger" for trigger step)

x.description: X coordinate for the step position

y.description: Y coordinate for the step position

edges.description: Optional array of connections between steps

source.description: The ID of the source step (use "trigger" for trigger step)

target.description: The ID of the target step

activate.description: Whether to activate the workflow immediately (default: false)

tools.name: create_workflow_version_step
tools.description: Create a new step in a workflow version. This adds a step to the specified workflow version with the given configuration. If parentStepId is not provided, the step will be appended at the end of the workflow.

workflowVersionId.description: The ID of the workflow version to add the step to

parentStepId.description: Optional ID of the parent step this step should come after. If not provided, the step will be added at the end of the workflow.

nextStepId.description: Optional ID of the step this new step should connect to

position.description: Optional position coordinates for the step

defaultSettings.description: Settings for the LOGIC_FUNCTION step. Must include input.logicFunctionId.

logicFunctionId.description: The ID of the logic function. Use list_logic_function_tools to discover available IDs.

workflowVersionId.description: The ID of the workflow version to add the step to

parentStepId.description: Optional ID of the parent step this step should come after. If not provided, the step will be added at the end of the workflow.

nextStepId.description: Optional ID of the step this new step should connect to

position.description: Optional position coordinates for the step

defaultSettings.description: Optional default settings for the step.

tools.name: update_workflow_version_step
tools.description: Update an existing step in a workflow version. This modifies the step configuration.

workflowVersionId.description: The ID of the workflow version containing the step

id.description: Unique identifier for the workflow step. Must be unique within the workflow.

name.description: Human-readable name for the workflow step. Should clearly describe what the step does.

valid.description: Whether the step configuration is valid. Set to true when all required fields are properly configured.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that this step connects to. Leave empty or null for the final step.

position.description: Position coordinates for the step in the workflow diagram.

logicFunctionId.description: The ID of the logic function that holds the code. This is auto-generated when a CODE step is created via create_workflow_version_step — do NOT set this manually.

logicFunctionInput.description: Key-value map of input parameters to pass to the logic function at runtime.

outputSchema.description: Schema defining the output data structure. This data can be referenced in subsequent steps using {{stepId.fieldName}}.

tools.name: update_workflow_version_trigger
tools.description: Update the trigger of a workflow version. This modifies the trigger configuration (e.g., changing trigger type, settings, or conditions).

workflowVersionId.description: The ID of the workflow version containing the trigger

anyOf.description: Database event trigger that fires when a record is created, updated, deleted, or upserted. The triggered record is accessible in workflow steps via {{trigger.object.fieldName}}.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

eventName.description: Event name in format: objectName.action (e.g., "company.created", "person.updated", "task.deleted", "company.upserted"). Use lowercase object names.

outputSchema.description: Schema defining the output data structure. For database events, this includes the record that triggered the workflow accessible via {{trigger.object.fieldName}}.

anyOf.description: Manual trigger that can be launched by the user. If a record is selected when launched, it is accessible via {{trigger.record.fieldName}}. If no record is selected, no data context is available.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

outputSchema.description: Schema defining the output data structure. When a record is selected, it is accessible via {{trigger.record.fieldName}}. When no record is selected, no data is available.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

tools.name: delete_workflow_version_step
tools.description: Delete a step from a workflow version. This removes the step and updates the workflow structure.

workflowVersionId.description: The ID of the workflow version containing the step

stepId.description: The ID of the step to delete

tools.name: create_workflow_version_edge
tools.description: Create a connection (edge) between two workflow steps. This defines the flow between steps.

workflowVersionId.description: The ID of the workflow version

source.description: The ID of the source step

target.description: The ID of the target step

sourceConnectionOptions.description: Optional connection options for iterator steps

tools.name: delete_workflow_version_edge
tools.description: Delete a connection (edge) between workflow steps.

workflowVersionId.description: The ID of the workflow version

source.description: The ID of the source step

target.description: The ID of the target step

sourceConnectionOptions.description: Optional connection options for iterator steps

tools.name: create_draft_from_workflow_version
tools.description: Create a new draft workflow version from an existing one. This allows for iterative workflow development.

workflowVersionIdToCopy.description: The ID of the workflow version to create a draft from

tools.name: update_workflow_version_positions
tools.description: Update the positions of multiple workflow steps. This is useful for reorganizing the workflow layout.

workflowVersionId.description: The ID of the workflow version

positions.description: Array of step positions to update

id.description: Step or trigger ID

tools.name: activate_workflow_version
tools.description: Activate a workflow version. This makes the workflow version active and available for execution.

workflowVersionId.description: The ID of the workflow version to activate

tools.name: deactivate_workflow_version
tools.description: Deactivate a workflow version. This makes the workflow version inactive and unavailable for execution.

workflowVersionId.description: The ID of the workflow version to deactivate

tools.name: compute_step_output_schema
tools.description: Compute the output schema for a workflow step. This determines what data the step produces. The step parameter must be a valid WorkflowTrigger or WorkflowAction with the correct settings structure for its type.

anyOf.description: Database event trigger that fires when a record is created, updated, deleted, or upserted. The triggered record is accessible in workflow steps via {{trigger.object.fieldName}}.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

eventName.description: Event name in format: objectName.action (e.g., "company.created", "person.updated", "task.deleted", "company.upserted"). Use lowercase object names.

outputSchema.description: Schema defining the output data structure. For database events, this includes the record that triggered the workflow accessible via {{trigger.object.fieldName}}.

anyOf.description: Manual trigger that can be launched by the user. If a record is selected when launched, it is accessible via {{trigger.record.fieldName}}. If no record is selected, no data context is available.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

outputSchema.description: Schema defining the output data structure. When a record is selected, it is accessible via {{trigger.record.fieldName}}. When no record is selected, no data is available.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

id.description: Unique identifier for the workflow step. Must be unique within the workflow.

name.description: Human-readable name for the workflow step. Should clearly describe what the step does.

valid.description: Whether the step configuration is valid. Set to true when all required fields are properly configured.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that this step connects to. Leave empty or null for the final step.

position.description: Position coordinates for the step in the workflow diagram.

logicFunctionId.description: The ID of the logic function that holds the code. This is auto-generated when a CODE step is created via create_workflow_version_step — do NOT set this manually.

logicFunctionInput.description: Key-value map of input parameters to pass to the logic function at runtime.

outputSchema.description: Schema defining the output data structure. This data can be referenced in subsequent steps using {{stepId.fieldName}}.

value.description: Whether to retry the action if it fails.
Whether to continue to the next step if this action fails.

workflowVersionId.description: The ID of the workflow version

tools.name: get_workflow_current_version
tools.description: Get the current version of a workflow. Returns the draft version if one exists, otherwise the last published version.

workflowId.description: The ID of the workflow to get the current version for

tools.name: update_logic_function_source

logicFunctionId.description: The ID of the logic function to update (from the code step settings.input.logicFunctionId)

code.description: The TypeScript source code for the logic function. Must export a main function.

tools.name: list_logic_function_tools
tools.description: List all logic functions marked as tools that can be added as LOGIC_FUNCTION steps in workflows. Returns their IDs, names, and descriptions.

message: Learned 15 tool(s): create_complete_workflow, create_workflow_version_step, update_workflow_version_step, update_workflow_version_trigger, delete_workflow_version_step, create_workflow_version_edge, delete_workflow_version_edge, create_draft_from_workflow_version, update_workflow_version_positions, activate_workflow_version, deactivate_workflow_version, compute_step_output_schema, get_workflow_current_version, update_logic_function_source, list_logic_function_tools.
